message:
fix(db): use inclusive expiry boundary when releasing expired reservations (br-rb2n)

The release_expired_reservations query used a strict less-than (expires_ts < ?)
comparison, which meant reservations expiring at exactly the current timestamp
were not released. This created a one-tick window where a reservation was neither
active nor released, causing inconsistency with the active-reservation check
(which uses expires_ts >= now, i.e. inclusive).

Fix: change to expires_ts <= ? so the boundary is inclusive and consistent with
the active-check semantics. Extract the WHERE clause into a shared constant
(EXPIRED_RESERVATIONS_WHERE_SQL) used by both SELECT and UPDATE to prevent
future drift. Add a compile-time assertion test verifying the inclusive boundary.

Also updates legacy Python submodule pointer and beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
